title: Ranginak (Date & Walnut Sweet)
description: A rich, no-bake traditional Southern Iranian dessert featuring soft dates stuffed with walnuts and blanketed under a toasted, spiced flour and butter mixture.

instructions.text: Carefully remove the pits from the soft dates. Stuff each date with a walnut half. Arrange the stuffed dates snugly in a single layer on a serving platter or shallow dish.
In a dry skillet over medium-low heat, toast the wheat flour, stirring constantly. Toast until it changes to a light golden brown color and smells nutty (about 10 minutes). Be careful not to burn it.
Reduce the heat to low. Add the butter and oil to the toasted flour. Stir constantly until the butter melts and forms a smooth, thick, paste-like roux.
Remove the skillet from the heat. Instantly stir in the cinnamon and ground cardamom.
While the mixture is still hot, pour it evenly over the arranged dates, spreading it to cover them completely.
Allow the dessert to cool to room temperature. Dust the top evenly with powdered sugar and sprinkle with crushed pistachios.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour to set. Cut into squares or diamond shapes to serve.

tips: Watch the flour closely while toasting; it can burn quickly. Use a fine mesh sieve to dust the powdered sugar evenly.
storage_notes: Can be stored at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 3 days, or refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.

substitutions.note: Can be used for a gluten-free version with a slightly different, nutty flavor profile.
Pecans fit nicely into dates and offer a similar texture.
